title: Tragacantha oocephala
taxonomy: Tragacantha oocephala is classified in the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Tragacantha is a subgenus of the genus Astragalus. Tragacantha oocephala is further classified as a species of the subgenus Astragalus and the genus Tragacantha. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants.
uses: Tragacantha oocephala, also known as needle bush or horse thistle, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and flowers are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and skin irritations. In some cultures, Tragacantha oocephala has been used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea. The plant's roots may also have a role in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Tragacantha oocephala for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Tragacantha oocephala, commonly known as the thorny acacia, does not have documented side effects, as it is often used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
